name: Smoked Fish Roe with Celery
intro: Bottarga, dried roe of mullet, is a prized ingredient in Sardinia and Sicily. The combination of the salty, fishy flavour of the bottarga with the fresh crunchiness of the celery is inspired.

ingredients.item: good-quality virgin olive oil, for drizzling

method: Peel off the very thin outer membrane of the mullet roe as it can be bitter.
Slice the celery stalks very thinly and arrange on individual dishes. Over the top of the celery, slice the bottarga very thinly.
Squeeze over the lemon juice, then a drizzle of virgin olive oil and some pepper and serve.
You can buy this fish roe at the fish markets or at your fish monger. The two long egg sacks are vacuum packed, so they will keep for a few weeks in the fridge.
